steps.title: Keep the title and name together
steps.content: The blank completes chemistry professor Yugang Sun and his colleagues as the subject of developed. A title directly before a proper name does not need a comma, and the name should not be separated from and his colleagues.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary commas
steps.content: Choice B incorrectly places a comma after Sun before the compound subject continues. Choice C separates both the title and the continuing subject with commas. Choice D incorrectly separates professor from the name. The choice with no comma in the title-name phrase or before and his colleagues is grammatical. The answer is A.
